findings.evidence: Grep hits: `BernsteinMeasures.lean:1415-1421` states the public lemma with `∀ n`; `BernsteinMeasures.lean:1438-1444` enters `Metric.tendsto_atTop`, chooses `N`, and only needs mass for `n ≥ N`. Mathlib uses the eventual form for the same sequence-level mass pattern at `Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/TightNormed.lean:264`: `(C : ℝ≥0) (hμ : ∀ᶠ n in atTop, μ n .univ ≤ C)`.
findings.fix: Change the hypothesis to an eventual uniform bound, e.g. `(C : ℝ≥0) (hmass : ∀ᶠ n in atTop, (σ n) univ ≤ C)`, and combine it with the kernel-convergence threshold in the proof.
findings.issue: `kernel_approx_error_tendsto` assumes `hmass : ∀ n, (σ n) univ ≤ ENNReal.ofReal C`, but convergence along `atTop` only uses the bound after the chosen threshold.

summary: One public convergence lemma asks for a global mass bound even though the atTop proof only needs an eventual one, which is the natural Mathlib shape for sequence-level measure bounds.
